Section 01
LED Board Management
The LED board displays live game data to your spectators and players in real time. Access and configuration is managed through the SmartPark portal under your complex settings.
LED Board
Uploading Rosters
Scheduling Games
Starting a Game
Tagging a Game
Troubleshooting
Best Practices
Spectator App
winreality.com
LED Board Configuration
Accessing Your
Board Settings
01
Go to My SmartParks
Log in to your operator portal and navigate to My SmartParks from the main dashboard.
02
Select Your Complex
Click into the specific complex where your LED board is installed.
03
Click Manage
Select Manage to access the full settings panel for that complex.
04
Click Configuration
Under Manage, open Configuration to access LED board display settings, layout options, and data field controls.
My SmartParks → [Your Complex] → Manage → Configuration
Path Summary
My SmartParks → Click Complex → Manage → Configuration. All LED board layout and display settings live in this panel. Changes apply immediately to the live board. Visit your portal at SmartParkops.com
★ WIN Tip
If you are a high school or college that has paid an annual fee, please disregard the "PAY" terminology. High schools and colleges will not be charged.
winreality.com/smartpark
Section 02
Uploading Rosters
Upload your team rosters before game time so player-level stats are correctly attributed to every pitch and at-bat throughout the game.
LED Board
Uploading Rosters

winreality.com
Section 02 — Rosters
Uploading
Rosters
01
Navigate to Rosters
Under Manage, click Rosters to access your roster management panel.
My SmartParks → [Your Complex] → Manage → Rosters
02
Create New & Bulk Upload
Click Create New, then select Bulk Upload to upload your roster via CSV file.
03
Designate Handedness
Ensure each player's glove hand and hitting handedness are designated in the roster upload.
⚠ Important
Rosters must be uploaded at least 12 hours prior to the start of a game.
★ WIN Tip
Add a player named *Placeholder* for both home and away teams. This allows you to start games without knowing the full lineup — adjust the lineup during the game as players arrive.
winreality.com/smartpark
Section 03
Scheduling Games
Build your game schedule in advance so rosters, LED board content, and spectator feeds are all ready before first pitch. Scheduling also unlocks the shareable event link for teams and fans.
LED Board
Uploading Rosters
Scheduling Games
Starting a Game
Tagging a Game
Troubleshooting

Spectator App
winreality.com
Section 03 — Scheduling Games
Creating Events
& Game Slots
01
Go to My SmartParks
Log in to your operator portal and navigate to My SmartParks from the main dashboard.
02
Click Into Your Complex
Select the specific complex where the game will take place.
03
Click Manage
Open the Manage panel for that complex to access scheduling and configuration tools.
04
Click + Schedule Event
Press the black + Schedule Event button and follow the on-screen prompts to enter event details, field, date, and time.
My SmartParks → [Your Complex] → Manage → + Schedule Event
05
Set the LED Configuration
During scheduling, make sure you select the correct LED Configuration for the event. This controls what is displayed on the scoreboard during the game.
06
Roster Upload Screen
After clicking through scheduling, you will be taken to the roster upload page. This is where Placeholders come into play — select the Starting Pitcher and Catcher to finish scheduling if the full roster is not available at that moment.
★ WIN Tip
Make sure game times are as accurate as possible. You cannot schedule two games at the same time on the same field.
⚠ Games must be scheduled & started for Spectator App access
Spectator App

Section 04

Section 04 — Starting a Game
Activating Live
Pitch Tracking
★ WIN Tip — Game Already Scheduled?
If the game is already scheduled, no action is needed to start it. The system will automatically start the game 15 minutes prior to game time. Follow the Scheduling Games instructions above.
01
Go to My SmartParks
Log in to your operator portal and navigate to My SmartParks from the main dashboard.
02
Select Your Complex & Field
Click into the specific complex, then select the field where the game is being played.
03
Click Manage
Open the Manage panel to access game controls for that field.
04
Click Start Game
Press Start Game and follow the on-screen prompts to complete game setup — assign teams, confirm rosters, and set game details.
My SmartParks → [Your Complex] → [Field] → Manage → Start Game
winreality.com/smartpark
Section 05
Tagging a Game
Tagging links pitch data to specific teams and players so reports and stats are attributed correctly. Tag live for the cleanest data and real-time spectator stats.

Section 05 — Tagging a Game
Linking Data to
Teams & Players
If Game Is Already Scheduled — Accessing the Tagging Feature
01
Go to Gameday
From your operator portal, navigate to Gameday to access all live and in-progress game activity.
02
Scorekeeping → Select Field
Click Scorekeeping, then click the correct field where the game is being played.
03
Game → Resume Game
Click into Game and select Resume Game to re-enter the tagging interface for the active session.
Gameday → Scorekeeping → [Field] → Game → Resume Game
★ WIN Tip — Keyboard Shortcuts
Clicking to tag is fine, but challenge yourself to use the keyboard shortcuts function — this will significantly speed up your tagging workflow. Press ENTER to save each pitch.
★ WIN Tip — Cross-Reference Every Inning
At the beginning of every inning, cross-reference the batter at the plate and the pitcher with the names shown in SPO to ensure proper data collection. To correct a name, click it and replace with the correct athlete.
winreality.com/smartpark
Section 06
Issues & Troubleshooting
Game-day problems are almost always fixable on-site. Use this guide to diagnose the most common issues quickly — and know when to escalate to WIN Reality support.
LED Board
Uploading Rosters
Scheduling Games
Starting a Game

Troubleshooting

winreality.com
Section 06
Issues & Troubleshooting
Issue Check First Fix
System off / not working Power disconnected at the outlet in the dugout Follow all cords and ensure nothing has been unplugged and all connections are tight. Check the outlet in the dugout first — this is the most common cause of a full system outage.
LED Board offline / no camera feed No pitches have been thrown yet, or camera feed was removed If no pitches have been thrown, continue — the board will activate once the game is started and the first pitch is tracked. If the camera feed was removed, re-add it in Manage → Configuration.
Pitches not being tracked Camera obstruction, machine balls in use, or turtle in pitching lane Ensure cameras are not moving and nothing is blocking the field of view. Clear the pitching lane — the turtle can sometimes inhibit tracking. Make sure the system is plugged in. Call support if the issue persists.

★ WIN Tip: The ball needs laces — machine balls do not always track reliably.
Spectator app not showing my field Game not scheduled or not started in SmartPark Ops Ensure the game has been scheduled and started in SmartPark Ops. Spectators cannot see a field unless a game is active. See Scheduling Games and Starting a Game sections.
ABS calls seem wrong Strike zone not configured correctly for the age/level Confirm the strike zone setup: My SmartParks → Complex → Manage → Configuration → scroll to Strike Zone Management and verify the correct profile is applied.

★ WIN Tip: Make sure strike zone is configured properly before all scheduled games.
Mobile game report not generating Game was not formally ended in SmartPark Ops Go to the game record and click End Game if it still shows as live. Reports generate within a few minutes of game end.
Cannot log in / keeps logging out Session expired or account access issue Log out fully, then log back in. If the problem persists, please contact WIN Reality support directly.
Support Line
For issues not resolved by the steps above, call WIN Reality SmartPark support: 512-956-7706. Have your complex name, field number, and a brief description of the issue ready.
winreality.com/smartpark
Section 07
Best Practices
Operators who follow these habits have fewer game-day issues, cleaner data, and better feedback from teams and coaches. Most of this is about preparation — the 15 minutes before first pitch matters more than anything that happens during the game.
LED Board
Uploading Rosters

Spectator App
winreality.com
Section 07 — Best Practices
Running SmartPark
The Right Way
✓
Upload rosters 24 hours before game time
Chasing roster uploads on game day is the #1 source of operator stress. Get them in the night before so you can focus on execution.
✓
Power cycle cameras and the LED board before your first game of the day
A fresh start clears any overnight connection drift. Do this 20 minutes before first pitch so the system is fully online before play starts.
✓
Confirm strike zone profiles match the age group before every game
Running a 10U game with an 18U strike zone profile will cause ABS disputes. Check this in Configuration before each new division plays.
✓
Publish your event schedule at least 48 hours before the first game
Teams and families need time to download the app and find their game. Publishing early drives spectator app adoption and reduces day-of questions.
✓
Always formally End Game when play is complete
This triggers game report generation and closes the data session. Games left "live" overnight can cause issues with the next day's tracking.
✓
Keep a backup device (phone or tablet) with operator access at the facility
If your primary device fails, you need another way to access the portal. Log in and confirm access on the backup before the event starts.
✓
Brief your staff on the spectator app before the event
Anyone at your gate or concession stand should know what the app is and how to tell a parent to download it. One sentence is enough: "Download WIN Reality to follow live pitch data from your kid's game."
winreality.com/smartpark
Section 08
Spectator Mobile App Activation
The WIN Reality mobile app is your spectators' live window into every game. Pitch speed, pitch type, ball/strike calls, and player stats — all on their phone in real time. Driving app downloads is one of the highest-value things you can do as an operator.
LED Board

Tagging a Game
Troubleshooting
Best Practices
Spectator App
winreality.com
Section 08 — Spectator App
Driving App Downloads
at Your Facility
What the App Shows Spectators
Live Pitch Data
Pitch speed, pitch type, and location on every single pitch — updated in real time as the game plays.
Ball / Strike Calls
Automatic ball and strike calls with a visual strike zone overlay. Every call is logged and reviewable.
Player Stats
Per-batter and per-pitcher stats that accumulate live throughout the game — tied to jersey numbers.
Event Schedule
The full published schedule for your event — game times, fields, matchups, and live status indicators.
How to Drive Downloads at Your Event
01
Post QR Codes at Entry and Concessions
Print and display a QR code linking to the app download at every entry gate and high-traffic area. Contact your WIN Reality rep for print-ready assets.
02
Announce the App on Your PA System
A 10-second PA announcement before games start is one of the highest-converting tactics: "Download the WIN Reality app to follow live pitch data from today's games — search WIN Reality in the App Store or Google Play."
03
Brief Gate and Concession Staff
Anyone interacting with families should know one sentence about the app. It doesn't require a pitch — just awareness and a point toward the QR code.
04
Deploy an On-Site Ambassador (Optional)
For larger events, a dedicated ambassador walking the venue and helping families download the app can significantly increase adoption. See your WIN Reality rep for the Ambassador Program guide.
✓ Operator Insight
Facilities that publish their schedule 48+ hours early and post QR codes at entry points consistently see 2–3x more app downloads per event. Spectator adoption drives long-term operator value — teams come back to venues where families can follow along.
winreality.com/smartpark
